MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-12-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Benjamin C. Graves; Law Offices of Benjamin C. Graves; 415-453-3600; appearing in person on behalf of plaintiffs Ronald Anthony Yates, et al.
Lawrence M. Scancarelli; Law Offices of Lawrence M. Scancarelli; 415-398-1644; appearing in person on behalf of defendant Kathryn R. Devincenzi.

Court reporter: Nancy Presant, CSR # 9906, npresantCSR@gmail.com, 510-757-8550.

Plaintiffs contest the tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED in its entirety. The only claim that plaintiffs seek to adjudicate is their first cause of action for partition. There is a triable disputes on that cause of action whether a one-third interest in the property sought to be partitioned is owned by Ronald Yates as a sole trustee or by Ronald Yates and Barbara Yates as joint as co-trustees and, if owned by the co-trustees, whether Ms. Yates is unavailable to perform her duties as co-trustee. Plaintiffs' separate statement, which is the due process document notifying Ms. Devincenzi what facts she must controvert to defeat the motion states that the one-third interest is owned by the co-trustees and makes no mention of Ms. Yates being unavailable to perform her duties as co-trustee. The evidence submitted with the reply papers was not considered. (Jay v. Mahaffey (2013) 218 Cal. App. 4th 1522, 1536-38). Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Nancy Presant, CSR # 9906, npresantCSR@gmail.com, 510-757-8550. (D501)
